# Copilot / AI Agent Instructions — personal_website_v2

Purpose: short, actionable guidance so an AI coding agent can be immediately productive in this repository.

1) Big-picture architecture
- This is a TypeScript React single-page app scaffolded with Create React App using `react-scripts`.
- Client entry: [src/index.tsx](src/index.tsx). App routing and page composition live in [src/App.tsx](src/App.tsx).
- UI layer: Material UI (MUI) with a theme in [src/theme.ts](src/theme.ts); animations use `framer-motion`.
- Visuals: `three` is used (see [src/components/ParticleBackground.tsx](src/components/ParticleBackground.tsx)).
- Build output: static site in the `build/` directory (and `public/` for unprocessed assets). Docker/Nginx and k8s manifests serve these artifacts.

2) Key files & where to make changes
- App shell & routes: [src/App.tsx](src/App.tsx) — add new pages under `src/containers/` and register routes here.
- Pages: `src/containers/` (LandingPage, About, Portfolio, Contact, Home).
- Reusable UI: `src/components/` (Menu, Footer, Transition, ParticleBackground).
- Theme & global styles: [src/theme.ts](src/theme.ts), [src/index.css](src/index.css), [src/App.css](src/App.css).
- Build / deploy: `package.json` scripts (see below), `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf`, and `k8s/` manifests.

3) Developer workflows (explicit commands)
- Install: `npm install` (or `npm ci` in CI).
- Run dev server: `npm start` (CRA dev server on :3000).
- Run tests: `npm test` (uses react-scripts test runner).
- Create production build: `npm run build` → outputs `build/` (used by Dockerfile/nginx).
- Linting: project uses CRA ESLint presets in `package.json` (no separate lint script).

4) Project-specific conventions & gotchas
- Folder separation: `components/` = presentational/shared; `containers/` = page-level/route components.
- Do not edit files inside `build/` — they are generated by `npm run build` and include hashed filenames (see `build/static/*`).
- Client-side routing: because `react-router-dom` is used, server must serve `index.html` for unknown paths. See `nginx.conf` and `k8s/ingress.yaml` for configured behavior — preserve this when changing server config.
- Theme toggling: App manages dark/light via `src/theme.ts` and a toggle in `src/App.tsx` — keep MUI theme usage consistent.
- Visual/three usage: `three` assets and components may be sensitive to SSR or hydration; prefer small incremental changes and test visually.

5) Integration points & dependencies to respect
- Major libs: `@mui/material`, `framer-motion`, `three`, `react-router-dom`, `notistack` (snackbars).
- Build & Docker: `Dockerfile` expects the production `build/` output (do not change build paths without updating the Dockerfile).
- K8s manifests in `k8s/` assume a static container image serving the `build/` output — update manifests when changing the image or ports.

6) Examples and patterns (copyable guidance)
- Add a route + page:
  - Create `src/containers/MyPage.tsx` exporting a React.FC.
  - Register route in [src/App.tsx](src/App.tsx): add `<Route path="/mypage" element={<MyPage />} />` inside `Routes`.

- Local dev quick-check after changes:
  - `npm start` → open `http://localhost:3000`
  - Verify route navigation and console for warnings (MUI/theme, three errors).

7) What to avoid changing without CI/preview
- Do not modify `react-scripts` version or eject unless you plan to adjust build/CI; eject is irreversible (`npm run eject`).
- Avoid manual edits to `build/`, `public/` index.html, and hashed assets.

8) Files to inspect for context when making non-trivial changes
- [package.json](package.json) — scripts and deps
- [src/App.tsx](src/App.tsx) — routing + theme toggling
- [src/index.tsx](src/index.tsx) — app bootstrap
- [src/theme.ts](src/theme.ts) — MUI theme definitions
- [src/components/ParticleBackground.tsx](src/components/ParticleBackground.tsx) — three usage example
- `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf`, `k8s/deployment.yaml` — deployment flow

9) When tests/build fail
- Reproduce locally: `npm run build` to surface production-only errors.
- Check console warnings in dev server for hydration or runtime problems (three.js often logs useful hints).

If anything here is unclear or you'd like more examples (CI, commit hooks, or component conventions), tell me which area to expand. Thanks!
